note: The script is characteristic of 10th century Melkite manuscripts. Two diagonal strokes are used for punctuation. Occasionally, one can see a diacritical dot under the letter /q/ — a fairly common feature in Melkite manuscripts (see, e.g., J. Monferrer-Sala, "Once Again on the Earliest Christian Arabic Apology: Remarks on a Palaeographic Singularity," Journal of Near Eastern Studies 69.2 (2010): 195-197

The Christian character of the Arabic undertext is proved by the occasional mention of "the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit" (fol. 114r, 117v). The text seems to focus on moral or ascetic issues (e.g., fol. 70r mentions "pure fasting"; fol. 118r mentions "those who consort with males")

bib.citation: Monferrer-Sala, Juan Pedro. "Once Again on the Earliest Christian Arabic Apology: Remarks on a Palaeographic Singularity." Journal of Near Eastern Studies 69.2 (2010): 195-197

cataloguer.message: Description and identification of undertexts as part of SPP (2011-2016). Migrated to SMDL in 2025.

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
